updates.title: Claude in Microsoft Foundry is Now Generally Available on Azure
updates.summary: Anthropic's Claude models — Opus 4.8 and Haiku 4.5 — are now generally available in Microsoft Foundry, running natively on Azure infrastructure powered by NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs. Enterprises can access Claude through their existing Azure accounts, with unified auth, billing, governance, networking controls, and an optional US data zone for data residency requirements. This marks a major shift from the earlier preview, with the 'Hosted on Azure' deployment option now fully production-ready while 'Hosted on Anthropic infrastructure' remains in preview.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed by official Anthropic blog post, Microsoft Azure Blog, and NVIDIA blog simultaneously published June 29, 2026. Multiple independent sources corroborate all key details. No red flags — this is a first-party announcement.

updates.title: Claude Code v2.1.196: Org Default Models, Trusted Devices, Security Hardening & UX Upgrades
updates.summary: Claude Code released version 2.1.196 on June 29, adding organization default models (admins set once; shows as 'Org default' in /model for users), Trusted Devices for Remote Control requiring device verification before viewing or steering local Claude Code sessions, and clickable file attachments (Cmd/Ctrl-click to reveal in Finder/Explorer). A significant security fix prevents claude mcp list/get from spawning .mcp.json servers in untrusted repositories — untrusted workspaces now show a 'Pending approval' state. The /code-review workflow was also improved, cutting token usage by approximately 25% by merging cleanup finders.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed directly from the GitHub releases page (anthropics/claude-code) with exact version v2.1.196 dated June 29, 23:27. All feature details match the official changelog. The security fix for untrusted MCP repos is a notable detail worth highlighting. Token savings figure (~25%) comes from the official release notes.

updates.title: Fast Mode Removed for Claude Opus 4.6 — Migrate to Opus 4.8
updates.summary: Anthropic removed fast mode support for Claude Opus 4.6. Requests to claude-opus-4-6 that include speed: 'fast' will no longer execute at fast speed or incur premium pricing — they will now run at standard speed and be billed at standard rates, with no error returned. Developers who rely on fast mode must migrate to Claude Opus 4.8, which is the currently supported model for fast mode inference.
updates.accuracy_notes: Consistent across multiple search sources covering the June 29 developer platform release notes. Straightforward API change. Main thing to verify: confirm exact date as part of June 29 platform release batch (high confidence based on context). No speculative claims.
